Pitcher-heavy slate.  Top-10 lineups stacked 3-4 pitchers (Yamamoto LAD 5.9, Ragans KC 5.0, Abel BAL 7.7, Singer CIN 2.7 w/3.0x, Misiorowski MIL 2.5) rather than the optimiser's V5.0 1P+4B shape — structural by design, not a bug.  Rank 1 (cadebrandt 70.92) was Yamamoto + Ragans + Abel + Singer + Goodman.  Mick Abel (BAL, 56 drafts, +0.8x boost) was the highest-RS pitcher at 7.7 — classic low-buzz TARGET pitcher archetype.  Buxton MIN (6 drafts, +3.0x, RS 5.9) was the top HV overall — ghost+max_boost thesis validated.  TARGET-batter archetype held: Wetherholt, Sosa, Kelly, Bogaerts, Goodman, Pereira, Murakami, Vilade, Bauers, Bregman, Guerrero Jr., Lindor all hit HV with <= 34 drafts.  FADE batters busted hard (as expected): Ohtani -0.2, Judge 0.3, Cruz -0.8, Pages -0.4, Schwarber 0.1, Henderson -0.7, J. Walker 0.5.  3x-boost batter trap held: Will Smith RS 0.2, Acuna 0.1, Alonso 0.4 all busted despite +3.0x.  Singer (CIN, 1800 drafts, +3.0x, RS 2.7) was the lone 3x-boost batter-adjacent name that paid off — and he is a pitcher, reinforcing the PITCHER_FADE_PENALTY 0.85 moderation.  No pop_factor / env_factor / matrix changes warranted — V6.2 backtest already validated cells within 3-5% over 20 dates; Apr 14 variance fits within that band.  Structural choice reaffirmed by user: keep 1P+4B anchor and MAX_PLAYERS_PER_TEAM=1.
